steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left of the blank: a possessive noun phrase serving as the subject (“John Donne's [noun]”). Right of the blank: a participial phrase (“much admired during his lifetime and in the decades that followed”) that precedes the main verb clause (“had... been gathering dust...”).

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for non-restrictive modifiers
steps.content: When a non-restrictive modifier comes between the subject noun and the main verb, it must be set off with commas. The correct answer is works, (choice C) because it provides the subject noun plus the comma that separates it from the descriptive phrase. The other options either introduce extraneous verbs (A and B) or create redundant verb structures (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
